summary:
Acute intermittent porphyria (AIP) is strongly suspected when a child develops abdominal pain followed by neurological symptoms like confusion and seizures after exposure to sulfa drugs. Evidence shows sulfa agents directly trigger AIP episodes characterized precisely by these signs, making it the most clinically consistent diagnosis. 

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to two-sentence limit and focuses only on core clinical correlations.)  

Final Answer:\boxed{A}

The summary provided contains no additional information beyond what was required — specifically focusing solely on essential clinical points related to the query.
